languages.text: "У Первого Призыва звание полковника было пожизненным — он уже остальные повышения и разжалования проводил. А потом его сын унаследовал звание, потом внук. Вот он-то и оказался слабаком. Прирезали его, а самый сильный и умный новым полковником стал. Он же ввел правило, что каждый, кто хочет новый чин, должен доказать, что достоин его. Провести тренировочный бой с другими такими же желающими. Кто в сержанта метит, с другими сержантами в корни уходит. Если вернулся, звание заслужил".

traits.locale_text: "У Первого Призыва звание полковника было пожизненным — он уже остальные повышения и разжалования проводил. А потом его сын унаследовал звание, а потом внук. Вот он-то и оказался слабаком. Прирезали его, а самый сильный и умный новым полковником стал. Он же ввел правило, что каждый, кто хочет новый чин, должен доказать, что достоин его. Провести тренировочный бой с другими такими же желающими. Кто в сержанта метит, с другими сержантами в корни уходит. Если вернулся, звание заслужил".

languages.text: "In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he granted all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. The rank passed to his son, then to his grandson, who turned out to be a weakling. The others slit his throat, and the strongest and most cunning of them took the colonel's position. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. It's the same for every rank: if you're aiming to become sergeant, you head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."
languages.original_text: "У Первого Призыва звание полковника было пожизненным — он уже остальные повышения и разжалования проводил. А потом его сын унаследовал звание, потом внук. Вот он-то и оказался слабаком. Прирезали его, а самый сильный и умный новым полковником стал. Он же ввел правило, что каждый, кто хочет новый чин, должен доказать, что достоин его. Провести тренировочный бой с другими такими же желающими. Кто в сержанта метит, с другими сержантами в корни уходит. Если вернулся, звание заслужил".

traits.locale_text: "In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he could grant all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. The rank passed to his son, then to his grandson who turned out to be a weakling. They slit his throat, and the strongest and most cunning took the Colonel's seat. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. Those aiming for a sergeant rank head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

"In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he granted all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. The rank passed to his son, then to his grandson, who turned out to be a weakling. The others slit his throat, and the strongest and most cunning of them took the colonel's position. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. Those aiming for a sergeant rank head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

"Well, in the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — the colonel had already received all other promotions and demotions. And then, somehow, it became a hereditary title. But that was ridiculous, as I'm sure you'll agree. It was impossible to oust hereditary colonels, and so that's when they switched to ritual fights to the death and 'training missions.' Well, after the death of the last colonel, I emerged victorious from a series of such missions."

"In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he could grant all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. The rank passed to his son, then to his grandson who turned out to be a weakling. They slit his throat, and the strongest and most cunning took the Colonel's seat. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. Those aiming for a sergeant rank head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

It's the same for every rank: if you're aiming to become sergeant, you head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

languages.text: 「さて、ファーストコールアップは終身で大佐の位に就いていた――昇進も降格も、それ以外はすでにすべて経験していたからだ。やがて、いつの間にかそれが世襲の称号になった。だが、ご存じのとおり、それは馬鹿げていた。世襲の大佐を追い落とすことはできない。そこで、命懸けの儀式的な死闘や『訓練作戦』へと切り替わった。最後の大佐が死んだ後、私はそうした作戦の連戦を勝ち抜いて、勝者となったのだ」
languages.original_text: "Well, the First Call-Up carried the rank of colonel for life — he had already received all other promotions and demotions. And then, somehow, it became a hereditary title. But that was ridiculous, as I'm sure you know. It was impossible to oust hereditary colonels, and so that's when they switched to ritual fights to the death and "training operations." Well, after the death of the last colonel, I emerged victorious from a series of such operations."

traits.locale_text: 「さて、ファーストコールアップは終身で大佐の位に就いていた――昇進も降格も、それ以外はすでにすべて経験していたからだ。やがて、いつの間にかそれが世襲の称号になった。だが、ご存じのとおり、それは馬鹿げていた。世襲の大佐を追い落とすことはできない。そこで、命懸けの儀式的な死闘や『訓練作戦』へと切り替わった。最後の大佐が死んだ後、私はそうした作戦の連戦を勝ち抜いて、勝者となったのだ。」

languages.text: « Lors de la Première Mobilisation, le titre de colonel était valable à vie, son détenteur pouvant promouvoir et rétrograder tout autre soldat à sa seule discrétion. Son grade s'est transmis à son fils, puis à son petit-fils, qui s'est révélé trop faible. Les autres lui ont tranché la gorge, et le plus fort et le plus rusé lui a succédé au poste de colonel. Dès lors, il a décrété que quiconque aspirait à une promotion devrait d'abord prouver sa valeur en affrontant ses concurrents dans le cadre de l'Épreuve par les armes. Il en va de même pour tous les grades : ceux qui briguent le grade de sergent se rendent dans les racines avec les autres candidats, et celui qui en revient a mérité ses galons. »
languages.original_text: "In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he granted all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. It's the same for every rank: if you're aiming to become sergeant, you head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

languages.text: "İlk Sevk Grubu'nda albaylık rütbesi, ömür boyu taşınan bir vazifeydi. Diğer tüm terfileri ve tenzilleri tek taraflı olarak gerçekleştirirdi. Rütbe oğluna, sonra da zayıf bir karakter olduğu ortaya çıkacak olan torununa geçti. Diğerleri torunun boğazını kesti ve içlerinden en güçlü ve kurnaz olanı, albaylık mevkisine geldi. Terfi isteyenin değerini kanıtlaması gerektiği kuralını koydu. Aynı hırsa sahip diğerlerine karşı Sınav Karşılaşması'na çıkarsın. Bu durum, her bir rütbe için geçerlidir. Çavuş olmak isteyenler, diğer adaylarla birlikte köklere gider. Geri dönen rütbeyi alır."
languages.original_text: "In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he granted all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. The rank passed to his son, then to his grandson, who turned out to be a weakling. The others slit his throat, and the strongest and most cunning of them took the colonel's position. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. It's the same for every rank: if you're aiming to become sergeant, you head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

Geri dönen rütbeyi alır."

languages.text: «Durante el primer llamado, el rango de coronel era vitalicio y tenía potestad para ascender y degradar a otros a su antojo. El título se legó a su hijo, y después a su nieto, quien resultó ser un pusilánime. Le rajaron el cuello, y el más fuerte y astuto de todos ocupó su lugar como nuevo coronel. Entonces dictaminó que, si alguien quería un ascenso, tenía que demostrar su valía enfrentándose a otros aspirantes en una prueba de combate. Es igual para todos los rangos: si quiere ser sargento, se adentra en las raíces junto con los demás candidatos. Si consigue volver, se ha ganado el rango».
languages.original_text: "In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he granted all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. The rank passed to his son, then to his grandson, who turned out to be a weakling. The others slit his throat, and the strongest and most cunning of them took the colonel's position. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. It's the same for every rank: if you're aiming to become sergeant, you head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

«Durante el primer llamado, el rango de coronel era vitalicio y tenía potestad para ascender y degradar a otros a su antojo. El título se legó a su hijo, y después a su nieto, quien resultó ser un pusilánime. Le rajaron el cuello, y el más fuerte y astuto de todos ocupó su lugar como nuevo coronel. Entonces dictaminó que, si alguien quería un ascenso, tenía que demostrar su valía enfrentándose a otros aspirantes en una prueba de combate. Los que aspiraban al rango de sargento se adentraban en las raíces junto con los demás candidatos. Si conseguían volver, se ganaban el rango».

languages.text: “在初代征召者时期，上校军衔本来是终身职位，掌管着军团所有的升迁和贬黜。后来这个职位传给了他儿子，再传给孙子，结果那孙子是个废物。于是其他人割了他的喉咙，其中最强大、最狡猾的人坐上了上校的位置。他定下规矩：想晋升，就得证明自己的价值。你必须和其他拥有同样野心的人在‘试炼比武’中对决。所有军衔都是这样——想当军士，就要跟其他候选人一起进入根系。只要能活着回来，这军衔就是你的。”
languages.original_text: "In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he granted all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. The rank passed to his son, then to his grandson, who turned out to be a weakling. The others slit his throat, and the strongest and most cunning of them took the colonel's position. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. It's the same for every rank: if you're aiming to become sergeant, you head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

traits.locale_text: “在初代征召者时期，上校军衔本来是终身职位，掌管着军团所有的升迁和贬黜。后来这个职位传给了他儿子，再传给孙子，结果那孙子是个废物。于是其他人割了他的喉咙，其中最强大、最狡猾的人坐上了上校的位置。他定下规矩：想晋升，就得证明自己的价值。你必须和其他拥有同样野心的人在‘试炼比武’中对决。所有军衔都是这样——想当军士，就要跟其他候选人一起进入根系。只要能活着回来，这军衔就是你的。”

languages.text: "Zu Zeiten der Ersten Einberufung war Colonel ein Posten auf Lebenszeit ... er sprach alle anderen Beförderungen und Degradierung eigenmächtig aus. Der Rang ging zunächst auf seinen Sohn und dann auf seinen Enkel über, der sich als Schwächling erwies. Die anderen schlitzten ihm die Kehle auf, und die stärksten und klügsten von ihnen nahmen die Position des Colonels ein. Von ihm stammt die Regel, dass man seinen Wert beweisen muss, um befördert zu werden. Ihr bestreitet den Prüfungskampf gegen einige Gleichgesinnte. Er ist für alle Ränge gleich: Wollt Ihr Sergeant werden, geht Ihr mit den anderen Kandidaten in die Wurzeln. Kehrt Ihr zurück, habt Ihr Euch den Rang verdient."
languages.original_text: "In the First Call-Up, the rank of colonel was a lifetime post — he granted all other promotions and demotions unilaterally. The rank passed to his son, then to his grandson, who turned out to be a weakling. The others slit his throat, and the strongest and most cunning of them took the colonel's position. He set the rule that if you want a promotion, you prove your worth. You fight the Trial Match against others with the same ambition. It's the same for every rank: if you're aiming to become sergeant, you head into the roots with the other candidates. If you make it back, you've earned the rank."

Von ihm stammt die Regel, dass man seinen Wert beweisen muss, um befördert zu werden. Ihr bestreitet den Prüfungskampf gegen einige Gleichgesinnte. "Zu Zeiten der Ersten Einberufung war Colonel ein Posten auf Lebenszeit ... er sprach alle anderen Beförderungen und Degradierung eigenmächtig aus. Der Rang ging zunächst auf seinen Sohn und dann auf seinen Enkel über, der sich als Schwächling erwies. Die anderen schlitzten ihm die Kehle auf, und die stärksten und klügsten von ihnen nahmen die Position des Colonels ein. Von ihm stammt die Regel, dass man seinen Wert beweisen muss, um befördert zu werden. Ihr bestreitet den Prüfungskampf gegen einige Gleichgesinnte. Wer Sergeant werden möchte, wagt sich mit den anderen Kandidaten in die Wurzeln. Kehrt Ihr zurück, habt Ihr Euch den Rang verdient."
